1.1: In the origin The Word had been existing and That Word had been existing with God and That Word was himself God.
1.2: This One himself was at the origin with God.
1.3: Everything was in his hand, and without him not even one thing existed of the things that existed.
1.4: In him was The Life and The Life is The Light of men.
1.5: And The Light is shining in the darkness, and the darkness did not overtake it.
1.6: There was a man sent from God; his name was Yohannan.
1.7: He came for a witness, to testify about The Light, that everyone by him would believe.
1.8: He was not The Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
1.9: For That One was The Light of Truth, which enlightens every person that comes into the world.
1.10: He was in the world, and the world existed by his hand, and the world knew him not.
1.11: He came unto his own, and his own received him not.
1.12: But those that received him, to them he gave authority to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his Name,
1.13: Those who had not been born of blood, nor of the desire of the flesh, nor of the desire of a man, but of God.
1.14: And The Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we beheld his glory, the glory as of The Only Begotten of The Father, full of grace and truth.
1.15: Yohannan bore witness of him and cried, saying, “This was he of whom I spoke: 'He that comes after me is preferred in honor before me, for he had priority over me.'
1.16: And of his fulness we have all received, and grace for grace.
1.17: For The Law was given by Moses, but grace and truth came by Yeshua The Messiah.
1.18: No man has seen God at any time; The Only Begotten God Who is in the bosom of The Father, he has declared him.”
1.19: And this is the testimony of Yohannan when the Judaeans sent Levites and priests to him from Jerusalem in order to ask him: “Who are you?”
1.20: And he confessed and did not deny and confessed: “I am not The Messiah.”
1.21: And they asked him again, “What, therefore? Are you Elijah?” And he said, “No.” “Are you a Prophet”, and he said “No.”
1.22: And they said to him, “And who are you? Tell us that we may give a statement to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
1.23: He said: “I am the voice that cries in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way of THE LORD JEHOVAH', just as that which Isaiah the Prophet said.”
1.24: And they that were sent were of the Pharisees.
1.25: And they asked him and said to him, “Why therefore are you baptizing, if you are not The Messiah, nor Elijah, nor The Prophet?”
1.26: Yohannan answered and said to them, “I am baptizing in water; but he is standing in your midst whom you do not know.”
1.27: “This is he who comes after me and he was preferred in honor before me; he whose sandal strap I am unworthy to loose.”
1.28: These were in Bethany at the crossing of the Jordan where Yohannan was baptizing.
1.29: And the day after, Yohannan saw Yeshua Who came to him and Yohannan said: “Behold, The Lamb of God who takes away the sins of the world!”
1.30: “This is The One of whom I said: 'After me a man is coming and he was himself before me because he had priority over me.'”
1.31: “And I did not know him, but so that he should be made known to Israel, therefore I have come to baptize in water.”
1.32: And Yohannan testified and said: “I saw The Spirit who was descending from Heaven like a dove and remaining upon him.”
1.33: “And I did not know him, but he who sent me to baptize in water, he said to me: “The One on whom you see The Spirit descending and remaining, This is The One who baptizes in The Spirit of Holiness.”
1.34: “And I have seen and have testified that This One is The Son of God.”
1.35: And another day Yohannan was standing and two of his disciples;
1.36: And he gazed upon Yeshua as he was walking and said: “Behold: The Lamb of God.”
1.37: And the two of his disciples heard as he spoke and they went after Yeshua.
1.38: And Yeshua turned and he saw them coming after him and he said: “What are you seeking?”, and they said, “Our Master, where are you staying?”
1.39: He said to them: “Come and see.” And they came and saw where he lived and stayed with him that day, and it was about the tenth hour.
1.40: One of those who heard Yohannan and followed Yeshua himself was Andrew, Shimeon's brother.
1.41: This one first saw Shimeon his brother and he said to him: “We have found The Messiah.”
1.42: And he brought him to Yeshua and Yeshua gazed at him and he said: “You are Shimeon, son of Yona; you shall be called Kaypha.”
1.43: And the next day Yeshua wanted to depart to Galilee and he met Phillipus and he said to him: “Follow me.”
1.44: Phillipus himself was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Shimeon.
1.45: Phillipus found Nathaniel and said to him: “We have found him of whom Moses wrote in The Written Law and in The Prophets; he is Yeshua Bar Yoseph from Nazareth.”
1.46: Nathaniel said to him: “Can anything good come from Nazareth?”Phillipus said to him, “come and see”.
1.47: Yeshua saw Nathaniel when he came to him and said about him: “Behold, truly a son of Israel in whom is no deceit.”
1.48: Nathaniel said to him: “From where do you know me?” Yeshua said to him: “Before Phillipus called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
1.49: Nathaniel answered and said to him: “Rabbi, you are The Son of God; you are The King of Israel.”
1.50: Yeshua said to him: “Do you believe because I said to you, 'I saw you under the fig tree? You shall see greater things than these.”
1.51: He said to him: “Timeless truth I speak to you all: From this hour you shall see Heaven being opened and the Angels of God as they ascend and descend unto The Son of Man.”

2.1: And on the third day there was a wedding feast in Qatna, a city of Galilee, and the mother of Yeshua was there.
2.2: And also Yeshua himself and his disciples were invited to the wedding banquet,
2.3: And it was lacking wine, and his mother said to Yeshua, “There is no wine for them.”
2.4: Yeshua said to her, “What do we have in common, woman? My hour has not quite yet come.”
2.5: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do.”
2.6: But there were there six watercasks of stone, set for the purifying of the Judeans, which held two or three nine-gallon-measures each.
2.7: Yeshua said to them: “Fill the watercasks with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
2.8: He said to them: “Draw out now and take to The Master of Ceremonies.”
2.9: And when The Master of Ceremonies tasted that water that had become wine, and did not know from where it was, ( but the servants knew, for they had filled them with water ) the Master of Ceremonies called the bridegroom,
2.10: And he said to him: “Every man first calls for the good wine, and when they are drunk, then that which is inferior, but you have kept the good wine until now.”
2.11: This first miracle Yeshua did in Qatna of Galilee and he manifested his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
2.12: After this he went down to Capernaum, he, his mother, his brothers and his disciples, and they were there a few days.
2.13: And the Passover of the Jews was drawing near, and Yeshua went up to Jerusalem.
2.14: And he found in The Temple those that were selling lambs, sheep and doves, and there were money exchangers sitting.
2.15: And he made for himself a whip from rope and cast all of them out of The Temple, and the sheep, the lambs, and money exchangers, and he poured out their money and overturned their tables.
2.16: And to those who had been selling doves he said: “Take these things out of here, and do not make my Father's house a house of trade.”
2.17: And his disciples called to mind that which is written: “The zeal of your house has consumed Me.”
2.18: But the Judeans answered and said to him: “What sign are you showing us that you are doing these things?”
2.19: Yeshua answered and said to them: “Tear down this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
2.20: The Jews were saying to him: “For forty six years this temple has been being built, and will you raise it in three days?”
2.21: But he said this concerning the temple of his body.
2.22: But when he had risen from the grave, his disciples were reminded that he had said this, and they believed the scriptures and the word that Yeshua had spoken.
2.23: But when Yeshua was staying in Jerusalem at Passover during the feast, many trusted in him because they saw the miracles that he performed.
2.24: But Yeshua did not entrust himself to them, because he knew all men,
2.25: And he did not need a man to testify to him about anyone, for he himself knew what was in a man.

3.1: One man of the Pharisees was living there; his name was Nicodemus. He was a leader of the Judeans.
3.2: This man came to Yeshua at night and said to him: “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher sent from God, for no man is able to do these miracles that you are doing unless God were with him.”
3.3: Yeshua answered and said to him: “Timeless truth I am telling you: If a person is not born again, it is impossible for that one to see the Kingdom of God.”
3.4: Nicodemus said to him: “How can an old man be born? Is it not impossible for him again to enter his mother's womb a second time and be born?”
3.5: Yeshua answered and said to him: “Timeless truth I am telling you: “If a person is not born from water and The Spirit, it is impossible that he shall enter the Kingdom of God.
3.6: That which is born from flesh is flesh, and that which is born from The Spirit is spirit.
3.7: Do not be surprised that I said to you that all of you must be born again.
3.8: The Spirit breathes where he will, and you hear his voice, but you do not know from where he comes and where he goes; thus is everyone who is born from The Spirit.”
3.9: Nicodemus answered and said to him, “How can these things be?”
3.10: Yeshua answered and said to him: “You are the Teacher of Israel and you do not know these things?
3.11: Timeless truth I speak to you: The things that We know We are speaking and the things that We see We are testifying, and Our testimony all of you do not accept.
3.12: If I have told all of you that which is in the earth and you are not believing, how shall you believe me if I tell you that which is in Heaven?
3.13: And no man has gone up to Heaven except he who went down from Heaven: The Son of Man - he who is in Heaven.
3.14: And just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, thus The Son of Man is going to be lifted up,
3.15: So that every person who believes in him shall not be lost, but shall have eternal life.
3.16: For God loved the world in this way: so much that he would give up his Son, The Only One, so that everyone who trusts in him shall not be lost, but he shall have eternal life.
3.17: For God did not send his Son into the world that he would condemn the world, but that he would give life to the world by him.
3.18: Whoever believes in him is not judged, and whoever does not believe is judged already, because he does not believe in The Name of The Only Begotten Son of God.
3.19: This is the judgment: The Light has come into the world and the children of men loved the darkness more than The Light, because their works were evil.
3.20: For everyone who does what is hateful, hates The Light and does not come to The Light, lest his works should be convicted.
3.21: But he who does The Truth comes to The Light, so that his works may be revealed, that they are performed by God.”
3.22: After these things, Yeshua and his disciples came to the land of Judea, and he was employed there with them and he baptized.
3.23: But Yohannan was also baptizing in Ainyon next to Shalim, because there was water there, and many were coming and were baptized.
3.24: For Yohannan had not yet gone to prison.
3.25: But there was a dispute between one of Yohannan's disciples and a certain Judeaen about purification.
3.26: And they came to Yohannan and said to him: “Our Rabbi, he who was with you at the crossing of Jordan, about whom you testified, Behold, he is baptizing and many are coming to him.”
3.27: Yohannan answered and said to them: “A man cannot receive anything of his own will unless it is given to him from Heaven.
3.28: You are bearing me witness that I said I am not The Messiah, but I am the one sent before him.
3.29: He who has the bride is the bridegroom, but the friend of the bridegroom who stands and listens for him rejoices with great joy because of the voice of the bridegroom. Therefore this, my joy, behold, it is full.
3.30: It is necessary for him to increase and for me to decrease.
3.31: For he who came from above is higher than all, and he who is from Earth is from the ground, and speaks from the earth. He who has come from Heaven is higher than all.
3.32: And whatever he has seen and heard he testifies, and no one is receiving his testimony.
3.33: But he who receives his testimony attests that God is true.
3.34: For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for it was not in a measure that God has given The Spirit.
3.35: The Father loves The Son and he has given all things into his hands.
3.36: Whoever is trusting in The Son, has The Eternal Life, and whoever disobeys The Son shall not see The Life, but the anger of God shall abide upon him.”

4.1: But Yeshua knew that the Pharisees had heard that he was making many disciples and was baptizing more than Yohannan,
4.2: When it was not Yeshua himself baptizing, but his disciples.
4.3: And he left Judea, and went on again to Galilee.
4.4: But it was necessary for him to come and pass through Samaria.
4.5: And he came to a Samaritan city called Shikar, beside the village that Jaqob had given to his son Joseph.
4.6: And Jaqob's spring of water was there, and Yeshua, weary from walking, sat down by himself at the spring at the sixth hour.
4.7: And a woman from Samaria came to draw water and Yeshua said to her, “Give me water to drink.”
4.8: For his disciples had entered the city to buy provisions for themselves.
4.9: And the Samaritan woman said to him, “How is it that you, a Jew, would ask me, a Samaritan woman, for a drink?” For the Jews do not associate with the Samaritans.
4.10: Yeshua answered and said to her, “If only you knew what the gift of God is, and who this is who says to you, 'Give me to drink', you would have asked for what he has, and he would have given you living waters.”
4.11: This woman said to him, “My Lord, you have no bucket and the well is deep. From where do you have living waters?”
4.12: Are you greater than our forefather Jaqob, he who gave us this well, and he drank from it, also his children and his flock? “
4.13: Yeshua answered and said to her, “Everyone who shall drink from these waters shall thirst again;
4.14: But everyone who shall drink of the waters that I will give him shall not thirst for eternity, but those waters that I give him shall be springs of waters in him that shall spring up into eternal life.”
4.15: This woman said to him, “My lord, give me from these waters that I shall not thirst again, and so I am not coming to draw from here.”
4.16: Yeshua said to her, “Go call your husband and come here.”
4.17: She said to him, “I have no husband.” Yeshua said to her, “You have said correctly, “I have no husband.
4.18: For you have had five husbands, and this man that is with you now is not your husband; this you have spoken truly.”
4.19: The woman said to him, “My lord, I perceive that you are a Prophet.
4.20: Our forefathers worshiped in this mountain, and you say that in Jerusalem is the place where it is necessary to worship.”
4.21: Yeshua said to her, “Woman, believe me that the hour is coming in which neither in this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship The Father.
4.22: You are worshiping what you do not know. We know what we are worshiping, for The Life is of the Jews.
4.23: But the hour is coming and now is, when the true worshipers will worship The Father in The Spirit and in The Truth, for The Father also is seeking such worshippers as these.
4.24: For The Spirit is God, and it is fitting that those who worship him worship in The Spirit and in The Truth.”
4.25: The woman said to him, “I know that The Messiah is coming, and when he comes, he will teach us all things.”
4.26: Yeshua said to her, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, I who am speaking with you.”
4.27: And while he was speaking, his disciples came and they were amazed that he was speaking with the woman, but no man said, “What are you seeking?, or, “Why are you speaking with her?”
4.28: And the woman left her cruse and went to the city and said to the men:
4.29: “Come see a man who told me everything that I have done. Is he The Messiah?”
4.30: And the men departed from the city and they came to him.
4.31: And in the midst of these things his disciples were begging him and were saying to him, “Our Master, eat.”
4.32: But he said to them, “I have food to eat of which you do not know.”
4.33: The disciples were saying among themselves, “Did someone bring him something to eat?”
4.34: Yeshua said to them, “My personal food is that I do the will of him who has sent me and finish His work.
4.35: Do you not say, 'After four months the harvest comes?' Behold, I say to you, lift up your eyes and behold the fields that are white and are ready to harvest even now.
4.36: And whoever reaps receives wages and he gathers fruit for life eternal and the sower and reaper shall rejoice together.
4.37: For in this is a word of truth, that 'One sows and another reaps.'
4.38: I sent you to harvest something in which you were not laboring, for another labored and you have entered upon their labors.”
4.39: Many Samaritans from that city believed in him because of the woman's saying which she testified: “He told me everything that I have done.”
4.40: And when those Samaritans came to him, they requested of him that he would stay with them, and he was with them for two days.
4.41: And many believed in him because of his word.
4.42: And they were saying to that woman, “Now it is not because of your word that we believe in him, for we have heard and we know that This One is truly The Messiah, The Lifegiver of the world.”
4.43: And after two days, Yeshua went out from there and left for Galilee.
4.44: For Yeshua had been testifying that a Prophet is not honored in his city.
4.45: But when he came to Galilee, the Galileans received him because they saw all the miracles that he did in Jerusalem at the feast, for they had also gone to the feast.
4.46: But Yeshua came again to Qatna of Galilee, where he had made the water wine, and a servant of a certain King was staying in Kapernahum, whose son was ill.
4.47: This man had heard that Yeshua came from Judea to Galilee and he went to him and was imploring him to come down and heal his son, for he was coming close to dying.
4.48: Yeshua said to him, “If you will not see signs and wonders, you will not believe.”
4.49: That servant of The King said to him, “My lord, come down or else the boy will die.”
4.50: Yeshua said to him, “Go; your son is saved.” And that man believed in the word that Yeshua spoke to him, and he went on.
4.51: But when he was going down, his servants met him and they announced good news to him and were saying to him, “Your son is saved.”
4.52: And he asked them at what time he was cured; they were saying to him, “Yesterday, in the seventh hour, the fever left him.”
4.53: And his father knew that in that same hour Yeshua said to him, “Your son is saved.” And he believed and his whole household.
4.54: This is again the second miracle Yeshua did, when he came from Judea to Galilee.

5.1: After these things there was a feast of the Judeans, and Yeshua went up to Jerusalem.
5.2: But there was a certain baptismal place in Jerusalem called in Aramaic, Bayth Khesda, and there were in it five porches.
5.3: And in these many people were lying who were ill, blind, crippled, cancerous, and they were awaiting the moving of the water;
5.4: For an Angel descended to the baptismal pool from time to time and moved the water for them; whoever first descended after the moving of the water was cured of all sickness whatever he had.
5.5: But a certain man was there who had been diseased for thirty eight years.
5.6: Yeshua saw this man lying, and he knew that he had been so for a long time, and he said to him: “Do you want to be cured?”
5.7: The sick man answered and said: “Oh, my lord, there is no one to put me in the baptismal when the waters are moved, but while I am coming, another goes down before me.”
5.8: Yeshua said to him: “Get up! Pick up your bed and walk.”
5.9: And immediately that man was healed, and he stood up, took his bed, and he walked, and it was the Sabbath day.
5.10: And the Jews were saying to him who was healed: “It is the Sabbath. You are not permitted to carry your bed.”
5.11: But he answered and said to them: “He who made me well, he said to me, 'Take up your bed and walk.' “
5.12: And they asked him: “Who is this man who said to you, 'Take up your bed and walk?'”
5.13: But he that had been healed did not know who Yeshua was, for he had withdrawn himself in the great crowd that was in that place.
5.14: After a time Yeshua found him in The Temple and said to him: “Behold, you are well again; do not sin, lest something worse than before should happen to you.”
5.15: And that man departed and said to the Jews that Yeshua was the one who had healed him.
5.16: And because of this, the Jews were persecuting Yeshua and were seeking to kill him, because he did these things on the Sabbath.
5.17: But Yeshua himself said to them: “My Father is working until this hour, and I am also working.”
5.18: And because of this, the Jews were especially seeking to kill him, not only because he broke the Sabbath, but also because he said that God was his Father, and was making himself equal with God.
5.19: But Yeshua answered and said to them: “Timeless truth I tell you: The Son cannot do anything of his own will, but the thing that he sees The Father is doing; for those things that The Father does, these also The Son does like him.
5.20: For The Father loves his Son and he shows him everything he does; greater deeds than these He will show him, that you may be astonished.
5.21: For just as The Father raises the dead and gives them life, thus also The Son gives life to them whom he will.
5.22: For it is not The Father who judges a man, but he has given all judgment to The Son.
5.23: That everyone should honor The Son as one honors The Father. He who does not honor The Son is not honoring The Father who sent him.
5.24: Timeless truth I speak to you: “Whoever hears my word and trusts in him who has sent me has the eternal Life, and he comes not into judgment, but he moves from death into Life.
5.25: Timeless truth I speak to you: The hour is coming, it is even now, when the dead shall hear the voice of The Son of God, and they who hear shall live.
5.26: For just as The Father has The Life in himself, so he has given also to The Son to have The Life in himself.
5.27: And he has given him authority to also do judgment because he is The Son of Man.
5.28: Do not be astonished at this, for the hour is coming when all who are in the graves shall hear his voice,
5.29: And they shall come out: those who have done good things, to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil deeds, to the resurrection of judgment.
5.30: I cannot do anything of my own will, but according to that which I have heard, I judge, and my judgment is just, for I am not seeking my will, but the will of him who has sent Me.
5.31: And if I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.
5.32: There is another who testifies about me, and I know that his testimony, which he testifies of me, is true.
5.33: You sent to Yohannan and he testified concerning the truth.
5.34: But I was not receiving the testimony of a man, but I say these things that you may live.
5.35: He was a blazing and shining lamp, and you were willing to boast about the time in his light.
5.36: But my testimony which is borne to me is greater than Yohannan's, for the works that my Father gave me to finish, those works which I have done testify for me that The Father has sent Me.
5.37: And The Father who has sent me, he testifies of me. You have never heard his voice and you have not seen his appearance,
5.38: And his word is not abiding in you, because you are not trusting in him whom he has sent.
5.39: Search the scriptures, for in them you hope that you have eternal life, and they testify concerning Me,
5.40: And you are not willing to come to me that eternal life may be yours.
5.41: I do not receive glory from the children of men.
5.42: But I know you, that the love of God is not in you.
5.43: I have come in The Name of my Father and you do not receive me, and if another shall come in his own name, you will receive him.
5.44: How can you believe, who are accepting glory one from another, and you are not seeking the glory of The One God?
5.45: Do you think that I am accusing you before The Father? There is one who accuses you: Moses, the one in whom you hope.
5.46: For if you had trusted Moses, you would also trust me, for he wrote about Me.
5.47: And if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”

6.1: After these things, Yeshua went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, the Sea of Tiberias.
6.2: And great crowds were going after him, because they saw the miracles he did for the sick.
6.3: And Yeshua went up to a mountain and sat down there with his disciples.
6.4: But the feast of The Passover of the Jews was drawing near.
6.5: And Yeshua lifted up his eyes and saw great crowds coming to him, and he said to Phillipus: “Where shall we buy bread that these may eat?”
6.6: But he said this as a test for him, for he knew what he was going to do.
6.7: Phillipus said to him: “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not enough, even if each of them takes a very little.”
6.8: One of his disciples, Andraus, Shimeon Kaypha's brother, said to him:
6.9: “There is a boy here who has five loaves of barley bread and two fish with him; but what are they to all of these?”
6.10: Yeshua said to them: “Have all the people be seated.” And there was much grass in that place and they were seated, the number of men being five thousand.
6.11: And Yeshua took the bread and blessed and distributed to them who were seated, and thus also from the fish, as much as they wanted.
6.12: And when they were full, he said to his disciples, “Gather the leftover fragments, that nothing be lost.”
6.13: And they gathered and filled twelve large baskets with fragments left over to them who ate from the five loaves of barley bread.
6.14: But those people who saw the miracle that Yeshua did were saying, “Truly, This is The Prophet who is coming to the world.”
6.15: But Yeshua knew they were prepared to come seize him and make him King, and he withdrew to that mountain alone.
6.16: And when it was evening, his disciples went down to the sea.
6.17: And they sat in the boat and were coming to the coast to Kapernahum and it was growing dark and Yeshua had not come to them.
6.18: But the sea rose up against them because a great wind was blowing.
6.19: And they drove about twenty five or thirty furlongs and they saw Yeshua as he was walking on the lake, and when he drew near to the ship, they were afraid.
6.20: But Yeshua said to them, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, do not be afraid.”
6.21: And they wanted to receive him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at that land to which they were going.
6.22: And the day after, the crowd that had stood at the shore of the sea saw that no other boat was there except that one on which the disciples had embarked, and that Yeshua had not entered with his disciples into the boat.
6.23: But other ships had come from Tiberias, next to that place at which they had eaten the bread which Yeshua blessed.
6.24: And when that crowd saw that Yeshua was not there, neither his disciples, they embarked these ships and came to Kapernahum and they were looking for Yeshua.
6.25: And when they found him at the other side of the sea, they were saying to him, “Our Master, when did you come here?”
6.26: Yeshua answered and said to them, “Timeless truth I speak to you: you seek me, not because you saw the signs but because you ate the bread and were filled.”
6.27: “Do not work for food that perishes, but for food that endures for the eternal life that The Son of Man will give you, for This One has The Father sealed as God with his seal of approval.”
6.28: And they were saying to him, “What shall we do to work the service of God?”
6.29: Yeshua answered and said to them: “This is the service of God, that you trust in him whom he has sent.”
6.30: They were saying to him, “What sign will you do, that we may see and believe in you? What sign will you perform?
6.31: Our forefathers ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written 'He gave them bread from Heaven to eat.' “
6.32: Yeshua said to them, “Timeless truth I speak to you: It was not Moses who gave you bread from Heaven, but my Father gave you The True Bread from Heaven.”
6.33: “For The Bread of God is he who has descended from Heaven and gives life to the world.”
6.34: They were saying to him, “Our Lord, always give us this bread.”
6.35: Yeshua said to them, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Bread of Life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger, and whoever trusts in me shall never thirst.”
6.36: “But I said to you that you have seen me and you do not believe.”
6.37: “Everyone whom my Father has given me shall come to me, and whoever will come to me I shall not cast out.”
6.38: “For I came down from Heaven, not to do my will, but to do the will of him who has sent me.”
6.39: “But this is the will of him who has sent me: I shall not destroy anyone from him whom he has given to me, but I shall raise him in the last day.”
6.40: “For this is the will of my Father: Everyone who sees The Son and trusts in him shall have eternal life, and I shall raise him in the last day.”
6.41: But the Judeans were murmuring about him for saying: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Bread, which has descended from Heaven.”
6.42: And they were saying, “Is not this Yoseph's son, whose father and mother we know? How does this man say, 'I have come down from Heaven?' “
6.43: Yeshua answered and said to them, “Do not mutter one with another.”
6.44: “No man can come to me, unless The Father who has sent me will draw him, and I shall raise him in the last day.”
6.45: “For it is written in The Prophets, 'All of them will be taught of God.' Everyone, therefore, who has heard from The Father and has learned from him, comes to me.”
6.46: “No man has seen The Father, except he who is from God; he himself sees The Father.”
6.47: “Timeless truth I speak to you: 'Whoever trusts in me has eternal life.'”
6.48: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Bread of Life.”
6.49: “Your forefathers ate manna in the wilderness and they died.”
6.50: “This is The Bread that came down from Heaven, that a man may eat of it and he shall not die.”
6.51: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Living Bread, who have come down from Heaven, and if a man will eat of this bread, he will live for eternity, and the bread that I shall give is my body that I give for the sake of the life of the world.”
6.52: But the Jews were arguing with one another and saying, “How can this man give us his body to eat?”
6.53: And Yeshua said to them, “Timeless truth I speak to you: Unless you eat the body of The Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in yourselves.”
6.54: “But whoever eats of my body and drinks of my blood has eternal life, and I shall raise him in the last day.”
6.55: “For my body truly is food, and my blood truly is drink.”
6.56: “Whoever eats my body and drinks my blood abides in me and I in him.”
6.57: “Just as The Living Father has sent me, and I am living because of The Father, whoever will eat me, he also will live because of Me.”
6.58: “This is the bread that came down from Heaven. It is not as your forefathers who ate manna and have died; whoever eats this bread shall live for eternity.”
6.59: These things he said in the synagogue when he taught in Kapernahum.
6.60: And many of his disciples who heard were saying, “This saying is hard. Who is able to hear it?”
6.61: But Yeshua knew in his soul that his disciples were murmuring about this, and he said to them, “Does this subvert you?”
6.62: “Truly you will see therefore The Son of Man ascending to the place where he was from the first.”
6.63: “The Spirit is The Life Giver; the body does not benefit anything. The words that I speak with you are spirit and life.”
6.64: “But there are men among you who do not believe”, for Yeshua himself knew from the first who they were who were not believing and who he was who would betray him.
6.65: And he said to them, “Because of this, I said to you that no man can come to me unless it has been given to him from my Father.”
6.66: Because of this saying, many of his disciples went back and were not walking with him.
6.67: And Yeshua said to the twelve, “Do you also wish to leave?”
6.68: Shimeon Kaypha answered and said, “My Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.”
6.69: “And we believe and know that you are The Messiah, The Son of THE LIVING GOD.”
6.70: Yeshua said to them, “Have I not chosen you twelve, and one of you is a Satan?”
6.71: But he said this about Yehudah, son of Shimeon Scariota, for he was going to be the one to betray him, being one of the twelve.

7.1: After these things, Yeshua was walking in Galilee, for he did not want to walk in Judea, because the Judeans were seeking to kill him.
7.2: And the Jewish Feast of Tabernacles was near.
7.3: And his brothers said to Yeshua: “Remove yourself from here and go to Judea, that your disciples may see the works that you do.”
7.4: “For no man does anything in secret and wants it done openly. If you are doing these things, show yourself to the world.”
7.5: For his brothers also did not believe in Yeshua.
7.6: Yeshua said to them: “My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.”
7.7: “The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I am testifying about it, that its servants are evil.”
7.8: “You go up to this feast; I am not going up now to this feast, because my time is not yet finished.”
7.9: He said these things and he remained in Galilee.
7.10: But when his brothers went up to the feast, then he also went up, not openly, but as secretly.
7.11: But the Judeans were seeking him in the feast and they were saying, “Where is he?”
7.12: And there was much murmuring in the crowd because of him, for there were those who said, “He is good”, and others were saying, “No, but he deceives the people.”
7.13: But no man spoke openly about him, for fear of the Judeans.
7.14: But when the midpoint of the feast had arrived, Yeshua came up to The Temple and he taught.
7.15: And the Judeans were astonished and were saying, “How does this man know the scrolls, having not learned?”
7.16: Yeshua answered and said: “My teaching is not Mine, but from him who sent Me.”
7.17: “Whoever is willing to do his will understands my teaching, whether it is from God or whether I speak for my own pleasure.”
7.18: “Whoever speaks for the pleasure of his own mind is seeking glory for himself, but he who seeks glory for the one who sent him is faithful and there is no evil in his heart.”
7.19: “Was it not Moses who gave you The Written Law? Yet no one among you keeps The Written Law.”
7.20: “Why are you seeking to kill me?” The crowds answered and were saying, “A demon is in you. Who is seeking to kill you?”
7.21: Yeshua answered and said to them, “I have done one work, and all of you are astonished.”
7.22: “For this reason Moses gave you circumcision, not that it is from Moses but because it is from the forefathers, and you circumcise a son on the Sabbath.”
7.23: “And if a son is circumcised on the Sabbath day because The Written Law of Moses should not be broken, do you complain about me because I have completely healed a man on the Sabbath day?”
7.24: “Do not judge with partiality, but judge just judgment.”
7.25: And the men from Jerusalem were saying, “Is This not he whom they seek to kill?”
7.26: “And behold, he speaks openly and they say nothing to him. Is it possible the Elders know that This is The Messiah?”
7.27: “But we know from where This One is. When The Messiah comes, no one will know from where he is.”
7.28: And Yeshua lifted up his voice as he taught in The Temple and he said, “You know me and from where I am, and I have not come of my own pleasure, but he who has sent me is true, whom you do not know.”
7.29: “But I do know him, because I am from unity with him, and he has sent Me.”
7.30: And they sought to seize him, and no man laid hands on him, because his hour had not yet come.
7.31: But many from the crowds trusted in him and they were saying, “When The Messiah comes, will he do more miracles than these which This One has done?”
7.32: And the Pharisees heard the crowds speaking these things about him and they and the Chief Priests sent guards to seize him.
7.33: And Yeshua said, “A little longer I am with you, and I will go join him who has sent Me.”
7.34: “And you will seek me and you will not find me, and wherever I am, you cannot come.”
7.35: The Judeans were saying among themselves, “Where is This Man prepared to go that we cannot be? Is He prepared to go teach the pagans?”
7.36: “What is this statement that he spoke?: 'You will seek me and will not find me, and wherever I am, you are not able to come?' “
7.37: But at the great day, which is the last of the feast, Yeshua stood and he proclaimed and said: “If a man is thirsty, let him come to me and drink.”
7.38: “Everyone who trusts in me, just as the scriptures have said, rivers of living water shall flow from within him.”
7.39: But this he spoke about The Spirit, Whom those who were trusting in him were being prepared to receive; for The Spirit had not yet been given, because Yeshua had not yet been glorified.
7.40: But many from the crowds who heard his words were saying, “This is truly The Prophet.”
7.41: Others were saying, “This One is The Messiah.” Others were saying, “Can The Messiah come from Galilee?”
7.42: “Has not the scripture said that The Messiah is coming from the seed of David and from Bethlehem, the village of David?”
7.43: And there was division among the crowd because of him.
7.44: And there were people among them who wanted to seize him, but no man put hands on him.
7.45: And those guards came to the Chief Priests, and the Pharisees and the Priests said to them, “Why have you not brought him?”
7.46: The guards were saying to them, “Never in this way has a man spoken as This Man speaks.”
7.47: The Pharisees were saying to them, “Have you also been deceived?”
7.48: “Have men of the leaders or of the Pharisees trusted in him?”
7.49: “However, this people who do not know The Written Law are damned.”
7.50: Nicodemus said to them ( he is one of them who had come to Yeshua at night ):
7.51: “Does our Written Law condemn a man unless one shall hear him first and know what he has done?”
7.52: They answered and said to him, “Are you also from Galilee? Search and see that a Prophet will not arise from Galilee.”
7.53: Then everyone went to his house.

8.1: But Yeshua went to the Mount of Olives, and in the morning he came again to The Temple.
8.2: And all the people came to him, and when he sat, he taught them.
8.3: But the Scribes and the Pharisees brought a women who had been seized in adultery, and when they stood her in the midst,
8.4: They were saying to him, “Teacher, this woman was taken openly in the act of adultery.”
8.5: “But in The Written Law of Moses, he commanded that we shall stone such as these.”
8.6: “What therefore do you say?” This they said, as they were tempting him, so that they might have something for which to accuse him.
8.7: But as they persisted asking him, he stood up and he said to them, “He among you who is without sin, let him first cast a stone upon her.”
8.8: And stooping down again, he wrote on the ground.
8.9: But when these heard, they were exiting, one by one, beginning from the Elders, and the woman who had been in the midst was left alone.
8.10: But when he stood up, Yeshua said to the woman, “Where are they? Has no man condemned you?”
8.11: But she said, “Not even one, LORD GOD”; and Yeshua said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, sin no more.”
8.12: And Yeshua spoke again with them and he said: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Light of the world. Whoever follows me shall not walk in darkness but shall find the light of life.”
8.13: The Pharisees were saying to him: “You are testifying about yourself; your testimony is not true.”
8.14: Yeshua answered and said to them, “Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, because I know from where I have come and where I am going, but you do not know from where I have come and where I am going.”
8.15: “You are judging carnally; I am judging no one.”
8.16: “But even if I do judge, my judgment is true, because I am not alone, but I and my Father who has sent Me.”
8.17: “And in your Written Law it is written: 'The testimony of two men is true.' “
8.18: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, I who testify about myself, and my Father who has sent me has testified about Me.”
8.19: They were saying to him, “Where is your Father?” Yeshua answered and said to them, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you had known me, you would have known my Father also.”
8.20: He spoke these words in the treasury as he taught in The Temple, and no man seized him, for his hour had not yet come.
8.21: Yeshua spoke again to them: “I am moving on and you will seek me and you will die in your sins, and where I am going, you cannot come.”
8.22: The Judeans were saying, “Will he now kill himself?”, because he had said, “Where I am going, you cannot come.”
8.23: And he said to them, “You are from below and I am from above. You are from this world; I am not from this world.”
8.24: “I said to you that you shall die in your sins, for unless you shall believe that I AM THE LIVING GOD, you shall die in your sins.”
8.25: The Judeans were saying, “Who are you?” Yeshua said to them, “Even though I have begun to talk with you,
8.26: There are many things for me to say and judge concerning you, but he who has sent me is true, and those things that I have heard from him, these things I am speaking in the world.”
8.27: And they did not know that he spoke to them about The Father.
8.28: Yeshua spoke again to them: “When you have lifted up The Son of Man, then you shall know that I AM THE LIVING GOD, and I do nothing for my own pleasure, but just as my Father has taught me, so I am speaking.
8.29: And he who has sent me is with me, and my Father has not left me alone, because I am doing always what is beautiful to him.”
8.30: When he was speaking these things, many trusted in him.
8.31: And Yeshua said to those Judeans who trusted in him, “If you will continue in my word, you are truly my disciples.”
8.32: “And you will know the truth, and that truth will set you free.”
8.33: And they were saying to him, “We are the seed of Abraham, and never have we served in bondage to a man; how do you say, “You shall be children of liberty”?
8.34: Yeshua said to them: “Timeless truth I speak to you: Whoever commits sin is a servant of sin.”
8.35: “And a servant does not always remain in the house, but a son always remains.”
8.36: “If The Son therefore will set you free, you will truly be the children of liberty.”
8.37: “I know you are the seed of Abraham, but you seek to kill me because you cannot comprehend my word.”
8.38: “I am speaking the thing I have seen with my Father; you are doing the thing that you have seen with your father.”
8.39: They answered and were saying to him, “Abraham is our father.” Yeshua said to them, “If you were children of Abraham, you would have been doing the works of Abraham.”
8.40: “But now, behold, you are seeking to kill me; I am a man who have spoken the truth with you, which I have heard from God; this Abraham did not do.”
8.41: “But you are doing the deeds of your father.” They were saying to him, “We are not from fornication; we have one father, God.”
8.42: Yeshua said unto them, “If God were your father, you would have loved me, for I have proceeded from God and have not come of my own pleasure, but he has sent Me.”
8.43: “And why do you not understand my word? It is because you cannot hear my word.”
8.44: “You are from your father The Devil, and the desire of your father you are willing to do; from the beginning he has been murdering men and does not stand in the truth because there is no truth in him; whenever he speaks a lie, he speaks from what is his, because he is of falsehood and is also its father.”
8.45: “But you are not believing in me, I who am speaking the truth.”
8.46: “Who among you is convicting me of sin? And if I speak the truth, why do you not believe me?”
8.47: “Whoever is from God hears God's words; therefore you are not hearing, because you are not from God.”
8.48: The Jews answered and they were saying to him, “Are we not saying correctly that you are a Samaritan and have a demon in you?”
8.49: Yeshua said to them, “A demon is not in me, but I honor my Father and you dishonor Me.”
8.50: “But I am not seeking my glory; There is One who seeks and judges.”
8.51: “Timeless truth I speak to you: whoever keeps my word shall never see death.”
8.52: The Jews were saying to him, “Now we know that a demon is in you; Abraham is dead and The Prophets, and you are saying, 'Whoever keeps my words shall never taste death.' “
8.53: “Are you greater than our father Abraham who died and The Prophets who have died? Who are you making yourself?”
8.54: Yeshua said to them, “If I glorify Myself, My glory is nothing; it is my Father who glorifies me, he of whom you say, 'He is our God.' “
8.55: “And you do not know him, but I know him, and if I had said that I did not know him, I Myself would have been a liar like you, but I do know him and I keep his word.”
8.56: “Abraham your father desired to see my day, and he saw it and rejoiced.”
8.57: The Jews were saying to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and you have seen Abraham?”
8.58: Yeshua said to them: “Timeless truth I speak to you: Before Abraham would exist, I AM THE LIVING GOD.”
8.59: And they picked up stones to stone him, and Yeshua hid himself and went out from The Temple and passed through their midst and moved on.

9.1: And as he passed, he saw a man blind from his mother's womb.
9.2: And his disciples asked him and they were saying, “Our Master, who is it that has sinned, this one or his parents, that he would be born blind?”
9.3: Yeshua said to them, “He had not sinned nor his parents, but that the works of God may appear in him.”
9.4: “It is fitting that I do the works of him who has sent me while it is day; the night is coming in which a man cannot work.”
9.5: “As long as I am in the world, I am The Light of the world.”
9.6: And when he had said these things, he spat on the ground and formed clay from his spittle and he smeared it on the eyes of him who was blind.
9.7: And he said to him, “Go wash in the baptismal pool of Shilokha”, and he went on, he washed, and as he was coming, he saw.
9.8: But his neighbors and those who had seen him begging before were saying: “Was this not he who sat and begged?”
9.9: Some were saying, “This is he”, and some were saying, “No, but he is someone like him”, but he said, “I am he.”
9.10: They were saying to him, “How were your eyes opened?”
9.11: He answered and said to them, “A man by the name'Yeshua' made clay and anointed me on my eyes and said to me, 'Go wash in the water of Shilokha', and I went, I washed and I saw.”
9.12: They were saying to him, “Where is he?” He said to them, “ I do not know.”
9.13: And they brought him, who before had been blind, to the Pharisees.
9.14: But it was the Sabbath when Yeshua made clay and opened his eyes for him.
9.15: And the Pharisees asked him again, “How did sight come to you?” He said to them, “He put clay on my eyes and I washed and sight came to me.”
9.16: And some of the Pharisees were saying, “This man is not from God because he does not keep the Sabbath”; but others were saying, “How is it possible for a man who is a sinner to do these miracles?” And there was a division among them.
9.17: Again they were saying to him who had been blind, “What do you say about him who opened your eyes for you?” He said to them, “I say he is a Prophet.”
9.18: But the Judeans did not believe concerning him that he had been blind and was seeing, until they called the parents of the man who was seeing.
9.19: And they asked them: “Is this your son, the same whom you were saying that was born blind? How does he now see?”
9.20: But his parents answered and said, “We know that this is our son and that he was born blind;”
9.21: “But how he sees now or who he is that opened his eyes for him we do not know. He has come of age, ask him, for he shall speak for himself.”
9.22: His parents said these things because they were afraid of the Judeans, for the Judeans had decided that if a man would confess him to be The Messiah, they would cast him out of the synagogue.
9.23: Therefore his parents said, “He has come of age; ask him.”
9.24: And they called the man a second time who had been blind and they were saying to him, “Glorify God, for we know that this man is a sinner.”
9.25: He answered and said to them, “If he is a sinner, I do not know, but one thing I do know: I was blind and behold, now I see.”
9.26: They were saying to him again, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes for you?”
9.27: He said to them, “I told you and you were not listening. Why do you want to hear again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
9.28: But they were insulting him and were saying to him, “You are his disciple; we are the disciples of Moses.”
9.29: “And we know that God spoke with Moses, but we do not know from where this man is.”
9.30: That man answered and said, “This is therefore to be marveled at, that you do not know from where he is and he opened my eyes.”
9.31: “But we know that God does not listen to the voice of a sinner, but listens to whoever worships him and does his will.”
9.32: “It has never been heard that a man has opened the eyes of one who was born blind.”
9.33: “If this one were not from God, he would not have been able to do this.”
9.34: They answered and were saying to him, “You were born entirely in sins, and are you teaching us?” And they cast him out.
9.35: And Yeshua heard that they had cast him out, and he found him and said to him, “Do you believe in the Son of God?”
9.36: He who had been healed answered and said, “Who is He, my lord, that I may trust in him?”
9.37: Yeshua said to him, “You have seen him, and he is The One who is speaking with you.”
9.38: But he said, “I believe, my Lord”, and falling down, he worshiped him.
9.39: And Yeshua said, “For the judgment of this world I have come, that those who do not see may see, and that those who see may become blind.”
9.40: And those of the Pharisees who had been with him heard these things and they said to him, “Are we also blind?”
9.41: Yeshua said to them, “If you were blind, you would not have sin, but now you say, 'We see.' Because of this, your sin stands.”

10.1: “Timeless truth I speak with you: whoever enters not by the gate to the sheepfold, but comes up from another place, is a thief and a robber.”
10.2: “He who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the flock;”
10.3: “To this one the gate keeper opens the gate and the flock hears his voice; he calls his sheep by their names and leads them out.
10.4: And when he has brought forth his flock, he goes before it and his own sheep go after him, because they know his voice.
10.5: But the flock goes not after a stranger, but flees from him, for it does not know a stranger's voice.”
10.6: Yeshua spoke this allegory to them, but they did not know what he was speaking with them.
10.7: But again Yeshua said to them, “Timeless truth I speak to you; I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Gate of the flock.”
10.8: “And all who had come were thieves and robbers, but the flock did not hear them.”
10.9: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Gate; if anyone will enter by me, he shall live and shall go in and out and shall find the pasture.”
10.10: “But a thief does not come except to steal, kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life and have whatever is abundant.”
10.11: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Good Shepherd. The Good Shepherd lays down his life for his flock.”
10.12: “But a hired man who is not a shepherd, whenever he may see a wolf coming, leaves the flock and runs.”
10.13: “But a hired man runs because he is a hired man, and cares not about the flock, and a wolf comes snatching at and scattering the flock.”
10.14: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Good Shepherd. I know mine and am known by mine.”
10.15: “Just as my Father knows me and I know my Father, and I lay down my life for the flock's sake.”
10.16: “But I have other sheep which were not from this fold; I must also bring them. They also will hear my voice, and the entire fold shall be one, and One Shepherd.”
10.17: “Because of this my Father delights in me, because I am laying down my life that I may receive it again.”
10.18: “No man takes it from me; I am laying it down of my own will, for I am authorized to lay it down, and I am authorized to receive it again; this commandment I have received from my Father.”
10.19: And again there was a division among the Judeans because of these words.
10.20: And many among them were saying, “There is a demon in him and he is raving mad, why are you listening to him?”
10.21: But the others were saying, “These are not the words of one who is possessed. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
10.22: But it was the Feast of Dedication in Jerusalem and it was winter.
10.23: And Yeshua was walking in The Temple on the porch of Solomon.
10.24: And the Judeans surrounded him and were saying, “How long will you keep our souls in suspense? If you are The Messiah, tell us plainly.”
10.25: Yeshua answered and said to them, “I have told you, and you do not believe, and the works that I do in the Name of my Father, they testify of Me.”
10.26: “But you do not believe, because you are not of my sheep, just as I said to you.”
10.27: “My sheep hear my voice and I know them and they follow Me.”
10.28: “And I am giving them eternal life, and they shall never perish, and no one shall snatch them from my hand.”
10.29: “My Father who gave them to me is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch anything from my Father's hand.”
10.30: “I and my Father, We are One.”
10.31: And the Judeans picked up stones again to stone him.
10.32: And Yeshua said to them, “Many excellent works from the presence of my Father I have shown you. For which of those works are you stoning me?”
10.33: The Judeans were saying to him, “It is not for excellent works that we are stoning you, but because you blaspheme, and as you are a man, you make yourself God.”
10.34: Yeshua said to them, “Is it not written in your law, 'I have said, “You are gods”'?”
10.35: “If he called those men gods because The Word of God was with them and the scripture cannot be destroyed”,
10.36: “Are you saying to The One whom The Father sanctified and sent into the world, “You blaspheme”, because I said to you, 'I am The Son of God?”
10.37: “If I am not doing the works of my Father, you should not believe me”,
10.38: “But if I am doing them, even though you do not believe me, believe those deeds, so that you may know and that you may believe that my Father is in me and I in my Father.”
10.39: And they again sought to seize him, and he escaped from their hands.
10.40: And he went on to the crossing of The Jordan, to the place where Yohannan had been before when he was baptizing, and there he stayed.
10.41: And many people came to him and were saying, “Yohannan did not even one miracle, but everything that Yohannan had said about This Man is true.”

11.1: And a certain man was sick, Lazar of the town of Bethany, the brother of Maryam and of Martha.
11.2: This Maryam was the one who had anointed the feet of Yeshua and wiped them with her hair, whose brother Lazar was sick.
11.3: And his two sisters sent to Yeshua and they were saying, “Our Lord, behold, he whom you love is sick.”
11.4: Yeshua said, “This sickness is not of death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified because of it.”
11.5: Now Yeshua did love Martha and Maryam and Lazar.
11.6: And after he heard that he was sick, he remained in the place where he was for two days.
11.7: Nevertheless, he said to his disciples, “Come, we shall go again to Judea.”
11.8: His disciples were saying to him, “Our Rabbi, the Judeans had been just now seeking to stone you, and are you going there again?”
11.9: Yeshua said to them, “Are there not twelve hours in the day? If a man walks in the daylight he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.”
11.10: “And if a man walks in the night he stumbles, because there is no light with him.”
11.11: These things Yeshua said and afterwards he said to them, “Lazar our friend is resting; I am going that I may awaken him.”
11.12: His disciples were saying to him, “Our Lord, if he is sleeping, he is recovering health.”
11.13: But Yeshua spoke about his death, and they had thought that he had been talking about lying down, taking sleep.
11.14: Then Yeshua said to them plainly, “Lazar has died.”
11.15: “And I am glad that I was not there for your sakes, that you may believe, but you should go there.”
11.16: Thomas, who is called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us go also and die with him.”
11.17: Yeshua came to Bethany, and he found that he had been four days in the tomb.
11.18: But Bethany was beside Jerusalem, separated from it by about 15 furlongs.
11.19: And many of the Judeans were coming to Martha and Maryam to comfort their hearts concerning their brother.
11.20: But when Martha heard that Yeshua had come, she went out to meet him; but Maryam was sitting in the house.
11.21: And Martha said to Yeshua, “My Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
11.22: “But even now I know that as much as you ask God, he gives to you.”
11.23: Yeshua said to her, “Your brother shall arise.”
11.24: Martha said to him, “I know that he shall rise in the resurrection in the last day.”
11.25: Yeshua said to her, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Resurrection and The Life; whoever trusts in me, even if he dies, he shall live.”
11.26: And everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die. Do you believe this?”
11.27: And she said to him, “Yes, my Lord, I do believe that you are The Messiah, The Son of God, who has come into the world.”
11.28: And when she had said these things, she went and called Maryam her sister, secretly, and she said to her, “Our Rabbi has come and has called for you.”
11.29: And when Maryam heard, she arose quickly and came to him.
11.30: But Yeshua had not yet come to the village, but he was in that place in which he met Martha.
11.31: But the Judeans also, who were with her in the house, who were comforting her, seeing Maryam rise quickly and go out, went after her, for they thought she went to the tomb to weep.
11.32: But Maryam, when she came where Yeshua was and saw him, she fell before his feet and she said to him, “If only you would have been here, my brother would not have died!”
11.33: But when Yeshua saw that she wept and those Jews who had come with her weeping, he was powerfully moved in his spirit and his soul was moved.
11.34: And he said, “Where have you laid him?” And they were saying to him, “Come see, Our Lord.”
11.35: And the tears of Yeshua were coming.
11.36: And the Judeans were saying, “See how much he loved him!”
11.37: But some of them said, “Was not this one who had opened the eyes of the blind man able to cause that even this one would not have died?”
11.38: But Yeshua, being powerfully moved within himself, came to the tomb, and the tomb was a cave and a stone had been placed over its doorway.
11.39: And Yeshua said, “Take away this stone.” Martha, the sister of him who had died, said to him, “My Lord, by now it is putrid, for it has been four days.”
11.40: Yeshua said to her, “Did I not tell you that if you would believe you would see the glory of God?”
11.41: And they took away that stone and Yeshua himself lifted his eyes above, and he said, “Father, I thank you that you have heard me.”
11.42: “And I know that you always hear me, but for the sake of this crowd that is standing here I said these things, that they may believe that you have sent me.”
11.43: And when he had said these things, he cried in a loud voice, “Lazar, come out!”
11.44: And he who had died came out, while his hands and his feet were bound in swathing bands, and his face was bound in a turban. Yeshua said to them, “Unbind him and let him go.”
11.45: And many of the Judeans that had come to Maryam, when they saw the thing that Yeshua did, believed in him.
11.46: And some of them went to the Pharisees, and they told them the thing which Yeshua did.
11.47: And the Chief Priests and the Pharisees were gathered, and they were saying, “What shall we do? This man is doing great miracles.”
11.48: “And if we allow him to do so, all the people will believe in him and the Romans will come and take away our position and our nation.”
11.49: But one of them, whose name was Qaipha, was The High Priest that year, and he said to them, “You know nothing,”
11.50: “And you do not consider that is profitable for us that one man should die instead of the nation, and not that the whole nation perish.”
11.51: But this he said not of his own accord, but because he was The High Priest that year, he prophesied that Yeshua was going to die for the nation.
11.52: And not only in place of the nation, but so that also the children of God who had scattered, he would gather together into one.
11.53: And from that day they planned to kill him.
11.54: But Yeshua was not walking openly among the Judeans, but he went from there to the region near Arabah, to a fortress city, which is called Ephraim, and there he was employed with his disciples.
11.55: But the Passover of the Jews was drawing near. Many came out from the villages to Jerusalem before the feast to purify their souls.
11.56: And they were seeking for Yeshua, and they were saying to one another in The Temple, “What do you think? Will he come to the feast?”
11.57: But the Chief Priests and the Pharisees ordered that if anyone knew where he was, he would show them so that they might seize him.

12.1: But Yeshua came to Bethany before the six days of the Passover, where Lazar was, whom Yeshua had raised from the grave.
12.2: And they made a supper for him there and Martha was serving and Lazar was one of the guests with him.
12.3: But Maryam took an alabaster vase of ointment of the best Indian spikenard, very expensive, and she anointed the feet of Yeshua and wiped his feet with her hair and the house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.
12.4: And Yehudah Scariota, one of the disciples, who was about to betray him, said:
12.5: “Why was not this oil sold for 300 denarii and given to the poor?”
12.6: He said this, not because he was concerned about the poor, but because he was a thief and he had the money box and anything which fell into it he carried.
12.7: But Yeshua said, “Let her alone, for she has kept it for the day of my burial.”
12.8: “For you have the poor with you always, but you do not have me with you always.”
12.9: And great crowds of the Judeans heard that Yeshua was there and they came, not because of Yeshua only, but also that they might see Lazar, him whom he had raised from the grave.
12.10: And the Chief Priests had deliberated that they would also kill Lazar,
12.11: Because many of the Judeans were leaving because of him and believing in Yeshua.
12.12: And the next day a great crowd which had come to the feast, when they heard that Yeshua had come to Jerusalem,
12.13: Took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him, and they were shouting and saying, “Hosanna! Blessed is he that comes in the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH, The King of Israel!”
12.14: But Yeshua found a donkey and sat down upon it just as it is written:
12.15: “Fear not, daughter of Zion, behold your King comes to you and rides on a foal, a colt of a donkey.”
12.16: But his disciples did not understand these things at that time, but when Yeshua was glorified, his disciples were reminded that these things were written about him and that they had done these things to him.
12.17: And the crowd testified that they were with him when he called Lazar from the tomb and raised him from the dead.
12.18: And because of this, great crowds went in front of him who heard that he had done this miracle.
12.19: And the Pharisees were saying to one another, “Do you see that you are not gaining anything? Behold, the whole world is going after him.”
12.20: But there were also some of them from among the Gentiles who came up to worship at the feast.
12.21: These came and approached Philippus, who was from Bethsaida of Galilee, and they asked him and were saying to him, “My lord, we wish to see Yeshua.”
12.22: And Philippus himself came and told Andreas, and Andreas and Philippus told Yeshua.
12.23: But Yeshua answered and said to them, “The hour has come for The Son of Man to be glorified.”
12.24: “Timeless truth I speak to you: Unless a grain of wheat falls and dies in the ground, it remains alone, but if it dies, it yields much fruit.”
12.25: “Whoever loves his life shall destroy it and whoever hates his life in this world shall keep it for eternal life.”
12.26: “If a man serves me, he shall come after me, and where I am there shall also my servant be. Whoever ministers to me, The Father shall honor.”
12.27: “Behold, now my soul is troubled, and what shall I say: My Father, deliver me from this hour? But for this I have come to this hour.”
12.28: “Father, glorify your name.” And a voice was heard from Heaven: “I have glorified and I am glorifying it again.”
12.29: And the crowd that was standing there heard, and they were saying, “It was thunder”, and others were saying “An Angel spoke with him.”
12.30: Yeshua answered and said to them, “This voice was not for my benefit, but for your benefit.”
12.31: “Now is the judgment of this world; now The Ruler of this world is hurled outside.”
12.32: “And when I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to myself.”
12.33: He said this that he might show by what death he would die.
12.34: The crowds were saying to him, “We have heard from The Law that The Messiah abides forever. How do you say that The Son of Man is going to be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
12.35: Yeshua said to them, “The Light is with you a little time longer. Walk while The Light is with you, lest the darkness overtakes you. Whoever walks in darkness does not know where he is going.”
12.36: “While The Light is with you, believe in The Light, that you might be the children of The Light.”
12.37: And whereas he did all these miracles before them, they did not trust in him,
12.38: That the word of Isaiah the Prophet might be fulfilled, which says, “My Lord, who is believing our report and to whom is the arm of THE LORD JEHOVAH revealed?”
12.39: Therefore they were not able to believe, because again Isaiah had said:
12.40: “They have put out their eyes and darkened their hearts lest they shall see with their eyes and understand in their hearts and should be converted and I would heal them.”
12.41: Isaiah said these things when he saw his glory and spoke about him.
12.42: Many also among the Leaders believed in him, but they were not confessing him because of the Pharisees, lest they would end up outside of the synagogue.
12.43: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
12.44: But Yeshua cried and said, “Whoever trusts in me, trusts not in me, but in him who sent me.”
12.45: “And whoever sees me sees him who has sent me.”
12.46: “I, The Light, have come to the world, that no one who believes in me shall abide in darkness.”
12.47: “And whoever hears my words and does not keep them, I am not judging him, for I have come, not to judge the world, but to give life to the world.”
12.48: “Whoever rejects me and does not receive my words has one that judges him: the word that I have spoken, that shall judge him in the last day.”
12.49: “For I have not spoken from myself, but The Father who sent me, he gave me commandments, what I will say and what I will utter.”
12.50: “I know that his commandments are eternal life, therefore, these things which I am speaking, just as The Father tells me, thus I speak.”

13.1: But before the feast of the Passover, Yeshua had known that the hour had arrived that he would depart from this world to his Father, and he loved his own who were in this world and until the end he loved them.
13.2: And when it was supper he cast Satan into the heart of Yehuda son of Shimeon Scariota, so that he would betray him.
13.3: And because Yeshua himself knew The Father had given everything into his hands and that he had gone out from God and he would go to God,
13.4: He arose from supper and put off his robe and took a towel and tied it around his waist.
13.5: He took water in a wash basin and began to wash the feet of his disciples and he wiped them with a towel which he had tied around his waist.
13.6: But when he came to Shimeon Kaypha, Shimeon said to him, “Are you washing my feet for me, my Lord?”
13.7: Yeshua answered and said to him, “What I am doing now you do not understand, but after this you will know.”
13.8: Shimeon said to him, “You will never wash my feet.” Yeshua said to him, “If I do not wash you, you do not have any part with me.”
13.9: Shimeon Kaypha said to him, “Therefore, my Lord, do not wash my feet only, but also my hands and my head.”
13.10: Yeshua said to him, “He who is bathed does not need to wash except his feet only, for he is wholly clean. You are also entirely clean, but not all of you.”
13.11: Yeshua had known who would betray him, therefore, he said, “Not all of you are clean.”
13.12: But when he had washed their feet, he took up his robe and reclined at the table and he said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?”
13.13: You call me, 'our Rabbi' and 'Our Lord', and you say rightly, for I am.”
13.14: If I therefore, your Lord and your Rabbi, have washed your feet for you, how much more ought you to wash one another's feet?”
13.15: For I have given you this example, that you also should do in the same way as I have done to you.”
13.16: “Timeless truth I speak to you: there is no servant greater than his master and no apostle is greater than he who sent him.”
13.17: “If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them.”
13.18: “I have not spoken about all of you, for I know those whom I have chosen, but that the scripture may be fulfilled, 'He who eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.',
13.19: “Now I am telling you before it happens, that when it has occurred, you shall believe that I AM THE LIVING GOD.”
13.20: “Timeless truth, I speak to you: whoever receives him whom I send receives me and whoever receives me receives him who sent me.”
13.21: Yeshua said these things and was powerfully moved in his spirit and he testified and said, “An eternal reality I tell you: 'One of you will betray me.' “
13.22: But the disciples observed one another because they did not know about whom he spoke.
13.23: But there was one of his disciples who was supported in his bosom, for whom Yeshua had great affection.
13.24: Shimeon Kaypha gestured to him to ask him, “Who is it of whom he spoke?”
13.25: And that disciple fell on the breast of Yeshua and said to him, “My Lord, who is it?”
13.26: Yeshua answered and said, “It is he for whom I dip the bread and give it to him.” And Yeshua dipped the bread and gave to Yehuda Bar Shimeon Scariota.
13.27: And after the bread, then Satan was brought into him, and Yeshua said to him, “What you are doing, do quickly.”
13.28: But no man among those reclining knew why he spoke to him.
13.29: For the men thought that because Yehuda had the money box that he had commanded him to buy something needed for the feast or that he would give something to the poor.
13.30: But Yehuda took the bread immediately and he went outside, and it was night when he left.
13.31: Yeshua said, “Now The Son of Man is glorified and God is glorified in him.”
13.32: “And if God is glorified in him, God also glorifies him in himself and at once glorifies him.”
13.33: “My children, a little longer I am with you, and you will seek me, and just as I said to the Judeans, 'The place to which I am departing, you cannot come', also I say to you now.”
13.34: “I give a new commandment to you: 'Love one another; just as I have loved you, you should also love one another.' “
13.35: “Every person will know by this that you are my disciples, if you shall have love one to the other.”
13.36: Shimeon Kaypha said to him, “Our Lord, where are you going?” Yeshua answered and said to him, “Where I go, you cannot now come after me, but at the end you shall come.”
13.37: Shimeon Kaypha said to him, “My Lord, why cannot I come after you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
13.38: And Yeshua said to him, “Will you lay down your life for my sake? Amen, amen, I say to you: 'A rooster will not crow until you deny me three times.'”

14.1: “Let not your heart be troubled. Believe in God and believe in me.”
14.2: “There are many lodgings in my Father's house, and if not, I would have told you, because I go to prepare a place for you.”
14.3: “And if I go prepare a place for you, I shall come again and bring you to join me, that where I am you shall be also.”
14.4: “And where I am going, you know, and you know the way.”
14.5: Thoma said to him, “Our Lord, we do not know where you are going and how can we know the way?”
14.6: Yeshua said to him, “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Way and The Truth and The Life; no man comes to my Father but by me alone.”
14.7: “If you had known me, you also would have known my Father, and from this hour you do know him and you have seen him.”
14.8: Philippus said to him, “Our Lord, show us The Father, and it is sufficient for us.”
14.9: Yeshua said to him, “All this time I am with you and you have not known me Phillip? Whoever has seen me has seen The Father, and how do you say, 'Show us The Father'?”
14.10: “Do you not believe that I am in my Father and my Father in me? The words which I am speaking, I am not speaking from myself, but my Father who dwells within me, he does these works.”
14.11: “Believe that I am in my Father and my Father in me, otherwise believe because of the works.”
14.12: “Timeless truth, I tell you: 'whoever believes in me, those works which I have done he will also do, and he will do greater works than these, because I am going to the presence of my Father.' “
14.13: “And anything that you will ask in my name I shall do for you, that The Father may be glorified in his Son.”
14.14: “And if you will ask me in my name, I shall do this.”
14.15: “If you love me, keep my commandments.”
14.16: “And I shall request from my Father and he will give you another Redeemer of the accursed, that he will be with you for eternity.”
14.17: “He is The Spirit of Truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it has neither seen him nor known him; but you know him, for he dwells with you and he is in you.”
14.18: “I shall not leave you as orphans, for I shall come to you in a little while.”
14.19: “And the world will not see me, but you shall see me; because I live, you also shall live.”
14.20: At that day, you will know that I am in my Father and you are in me and I am in you.”
14.21: “Whoever has my commands and keeps them, he does love me, but he who loves me shall be loved from my Father and I shall love him, and I shall show myself to him.”
14.22: Yehuda said to him (he was not Scariota), “My Lord, how is it you are going to show yourself to us and not the same to the world?”
14.23: Yeshua answered and said to him, “Whoever loves me keeps my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him, and we will make our lodging with him.”
14.24: “But he who does not love me does not keep my word, and this word which you are hearing is not mine, but The Father's who has sent me.”
14.25: “I have spoken these things with you while I am with you.”
14.26: “But he, The Redeemer of the accursed, The Spirit of Holiness, whom my Father sends in my name, he will teach you all things and he will remind you of everything whatsoever I have told you.”
14.27: “Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. It is not as the world gives that I give to you. Do not let your heart be troubled, and do not let it be afraid.”
14.28: “You have heard that I said to you, 'I am going away, and I am coming to you'; if you had loved me, you would have rejoiced that I am going to join my Father, for my Father is greater than I.”
14.29: “And now, behold, I have told you before it happens, that when it has happened, you may believe.”
14.30: “After this I will not be speaking much with you, for The Prince of the world is coming and he has nothing to use against me.
14.31: But that the world may know that I love my Father, and just as my Father has taught me, so I have done. Rise up, let us depart from here.”

15.1: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The True Vine, and my Father is the vine dresser.”
15.2: “Every branch on me not yielding fruit he takes away, and that which yields fruit he purges that it may bring forth much fruit.”
15.3: “From now on you are purged because of the word which I have spoken with you.”
15.4: “Stay with me, and I am in you. Just as the branch cannot yield fruit by itself unless it remains on the vine, so neither do you unless you stay with me.”
15.5: “I AM THE LIVING GOD, The Vine, and you are the branches; whoever abides with me and I in him, this one brings forth much fruit, because without me, you can do nothing.”
15.6: “If a man does not abide with me, he is thrown away like a shriveled up branch, and they gather it, throwing it into the fire to burn.”
15.7: “But if you are abiding in me and my words will abide in you, everything whatsoever you desire to ask, it shall be done for you.”
15.8: “In this The Father is glorified, that you will bring forth much fruit and that you will be my disciples.”
15.9: “Just as my Father has loved me, even so I have loved you; continue in my friendship.”
15.10: “If you keep my commands, you will remain in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commands and I abide in his love.”
15.11: “I have spoken these things with you that my joy may be in you and that your joy may be perfect.”
15.12: “This is my commandment, that you love one another, just as I have loved you.”
15.13: “There is no greater love than this: that a person would lay down his life for the sake of his friends.”
15.14: “You are my friends if you will do all that I command you.”
15.15: “No longer do I call you servants, because a servant does not know what his master does, but I have called you my friends, because all that I have heard from my Father, I have taught you.”
15.16: “You have not chosen me, but I have chosen you and I have appointed you so that you also will go bring forth fruit and your fruit will remain, so that all you will ask my Father in my name, he will give to you.”
15.17: “These things I command you that you will love one another.”
15.18: “And if the world hates you, know that it hated me before you.”
